rules.name: Native Technologies Only
rules.description: Use only C++20 standard library, Windows SDK, WinUI 3 (C++/WinRT), and Microsoft-provided tools (vcpkg, MSVC, CppUnitTest). nlohmann/json via vcpkg is allowed. No Boost, no non-Microsoft third-party tools.

rules.name: Sealed Framework
rules.description: ForsettiCore, ForsettiPlatform, and ForsettiHostTemplate are sealed static libraries. Consumers use public headers only. No subclassing framework classes unless explicitly designed for extension.

rules.name: No Internal Patching
rules.description: Never modify internal implementation files of the framework libraries. Extend functionality through public interfaces (abstract base classes) only.

rules.name: Public APIs Only
rules.description: Modules interact with the framework exclusively through published interfaces (abstract base classes in include/ headers).

rules.name: Strict OOP
rules.description: Interface-first design (abstract base classes with pure virtuals). Constructor dependency injection — no hidden globals. All classes must be 'final' unless extension is intentional and documented.

rules.name: One-Way Dependencies
rules.description: ForsettiCore depends on nothing. ForsettiPlatform depends on Core. ForsettiHostTemplate depends on Core + Platform. ForsettiModulesExample depends on Core only. No reverse or lateral imports.

rules.name: Run Verification
rules.description: Every PR runs Scripts/verify-forsetti-guardrails.ps1 which executes cmake build, ctest, and architecture enforcement checks.

rules.name: Proprietary Licensing
rules.description: All source files carry proprietary header comment. Copyright James Daley 2026. All Rights Reserved.

rules.name: CI Build Parity
rules.description: CI pipeline (GitHub Actions on windows-latest) must match local build configuration exactly.

framework_behavior_invariants: Only one UI module may be active at a time
Service modules may run concurrently without limit
Module activation requires passing compatibility check AND entitlement check
UIThemeMask capability is reserved for the framework — modules must not request it
Module communication guard blocks: empty IDs, self-messaging, forsetti.internal.* namespace
UISurfaceManager sanitizes module UI contributions (strips themeMask)
